title: Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) v0.1
description: A standard, machine-readable format for design system documentation covering components, design tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and patterns. A DSDS document contains one or more document groups — named collections of typed artifact arrays (components, tokens, tokenGroups, themes, styles, patterns). Individual artifacts are defined in their respective sub-schemas: components/ (component, anatomy, api, design-specifications, variant, state), tokens/ (token, token-group, token-value, token-api, theme), style/ (style), and patterns/ (pattern).

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The version of the DSDS specification this document conforms to.

documentGroups.description: One or more document groups. Each group is a named collection of typed artifacts — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns. Multiple groups allow a single DSDS file to organize artifacts into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components).

documentGroup.description: A named collection of design system documentation artifacts. Contains one or more arrays of typed artifacts — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns. At least one artifact array must be present.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation').

tokens.description: Array of individual token documentation objects.

tokenGroups.description: Array of token group documentation objects. Each group can contain tokens, nested groups, or a mix of both, forming an arbitrarily deep hierarchy.

themes.description: Array of theme documentation objects. Each theme provides alternative token values for a named context such as a color mode, density setting, or brand variant.
